Name: efficient_inference_optimization
Title: Quantization-Aware Spectral Latent Steering for Efficient Language Model Inference
Short Hypothesis: By integrating quantization techniques with spectral latent steering, we can achieve efficient language model inference with minimal loss in accuracy, leveraging the strengths of both methods to optimize for latency and memory usage on commodity GPUs.
Related Work: Recent works such as Memory-Efficient Speculative Decoding and Compiling Code LLMs into Lightweight Executables have explored efficient inference techniques for language models. Our proposal builds upon these efforts by combining quantization with spectral latent steering, offering a novel approach to optimize inference efficiency while maintaining accuracy. Unlike Spectral Latent Steering, which focuses solely on test-time optimization, our method incorporates quantization to further reduce computational overhead.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework that integrates quantization techniques with spectral latent steering to optimize language model inference efficiency. By performing a single-pass PCA on initial layer activations and projecting subsequent updates onto a low-rank subspace, we reduce computational overhead while preserving optimization landscape geometry. Quantization is applied to the projected subspace to further decrease memory usage and latency. Our approach achieves a significant speedup in token generation throughput and reduces memory usage, making it suitable for deployment on commodity GPUs. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in maintaining accuracy while optimizing for efficiency.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed framework on a range of language models, including LLaMA-7B and Code LLMs
Compare the performance of our method with existing efficient inference techniques, such as Memory-Efficient Speculative Decoding and Compiling Code LLMs into Lightweight Executables
Measure the impact of quantization on accuracy and latency, and explore different quantization techniques to optimize the trade-off between efficiency and accuracy
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the contributions of spectral latent steering and quantization to the overall efficiency gains
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential loss in accuracy due to quantization
Increased computational overhead for spectral latent steering
Limited applicability to certain language models or tasks
Requires careful tuning of hyperparameters to optimize efficiency and accuracy trade-off
